senses.definition: things that can be easily broken or are fragile
examples: The shop sells a variety of breakables in glass and porcelain.
Be careful with the breakables while moving the boxes.

senses.definition: in a way that can be broken easily
examples: Glass is breakably thin in this section.
The vase is breakably stored in soft padding.

senses.definition: the act or result of something breaking; a broken piece
examples: There was a small breakage in the glassware.
Note the breakage caused by the fall.

senses.definition: plural form of breakage; multiple instances of things breaking
examples: The warehouse reported several breakages after the move.
We covered the breakages under insurance.

senses.definition: a separation or escape from a larger group or organization
examples: The protest leader attempted a breakaway from the main march.
A breakaway group formed after disagreements.

senses.definition: able to detach or move away easily
examples: The breakaway switch is designed to be breakaway for safety.
Breakaway cables prevent entanglement.

senses.definition: plural of breakaway; separate groups or detachable items
examples: Several breakaways formed during the meeting.
The headband fasteners are breakaways.

senses.definition: an unusual or dialect term; not widely recognized in standard English; may refer to a tool or brand name
examples: Look for the breakax tool in the hardware aisle.

senses.definition: a tool for breaking or splitting, similar to an axe; may be a dialect/brand term
examples: He used a breakaxe to split the log.

senses.definition: a return to a previous state or a setback; a specific sports term (e.g., in racing or climbing)
examples: The team conceded a breakback after halftime.
A sudden breakback in the negotiation changed the mood.

senses.definition: a short, rhythmic drum loop sampled from earlier music; a style used in hip-hop and electronic music
examples: The producer used a classic breakbeat in the track.
Breakbeats drive the groove.

senses.definition: plural of breakbeat; multiple drum loops or tracks used in music production
examples: The catalog includes several breakbeats for producers.
They sampled old breakbeats from vinyl.

senses.definition: a disease nickname historically used for dengue fever (bone-breaking fever); now uncommon and potentially outdated
examples: Historically, dengue fever was called breakbone fever.

senses.definition: plural form of breakbone; used rarely in medical or historical contexts
examples: Medical notes mention breakbones in elderly patients.

senses.definition: a style of dancing to breakbeat music with acrobatic moves
examples: She practices breakdance every evening.
The breakdance crew performed at the festival.

senses.definition: to perform breakdance moves
examples: They breakdanced all night at the club.
He breaks and spins on the floor.

senses.definition: past tense of breakdance; performed breakdance moves
examples: She breakdanced on stage yesterday.
They breakdanced to the beat all night.

senses.definition: a person who performs breakdance
examples: He's a famous breakdancer.

senses.definition: plural of breakdancer; multiple dancers who perform breakdance
examples: The breakdancers wowed the crowd.
Two breakdancers joined the show.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of breakdance; performs breakdance moves
examples: She breakdances to express herself.
He breakdances better than anyone else here.

senses.definition: the art or practice of breakdancing
examples: Breakdancing requires balance and creativity.
She specializes in breakdancing and street performances.

senses.definition: plural form referring to multiple breakdancing sessions or movements; uncommon usage
examples: They watched several breakdancings from the crew.

senses.definition: a failure or collapse; a detailed analysis or description; a temporary loss of function
examples: The machine had a breakdown and stopped working.
We did a breakdown of the costs in the report.

senses.definition: plural of breakdown; multiple failures or analyses
examples: There were several breakdowns in the system.
We reviewed the breakdowns in spending.

senses.definition: a person or thing that breaks; a device to interrupt current; a person who breaks rocks or waves
examples: He is a fast breaker of code.
The breaker cut the current during the storm.

senses.definition: rare/unknown term; possibly a surname or fictional role; not standard
examples: The character is Breakerman in the game.

senses.definition: plural of Breakerman; fictional or occupational plural; not common
examples: The team included several breakermen in the story.
